elements.title: Perth County Courthouse
elements.contents: ... that the Perth County Courthouse (pictured) was built after a judge refused to hold hearings in its predecessor building?

elements.title: Chaerul Umam
elements.contents: ... that two films by Chaerul Umam – Ketika Cinta Bertasbih and its sequel – were the first to be labelled halal by the Indonesian Ulama Council?

elements.title: The Poison of Polygamy
elements.contents: ... that the novel The Poison of Polygamy was rediscovered almost a century after its publication by a researcher studying early Chinese-language Australian newspapers?

elements.title: Finity
elements.contents: ... that the science-fiction novel Finity was praised by reviewers as an "adventure of ideas", with inventive use of virtual reality and parallel-world concepts despite its thin plot?

elements.title: Zirkova
elements.contents: ... that the vodka brand Zirkova raised funds for Ukraine after the Russian invasion, and brought its master distiller to Canada to continue production?

elements.title: Albert Einstein debated the nature of time with the "most famous philosopher" alive
elements.contents: ... that Albert Einstein debated the nature of time with the "most famous philosopher" alive in 1922?

elements.title: Moergito
elements.contents: ... that Indonesian military leader Moergito banned the production of machetes in East Java shortly after the government ordered twenty thousand of them?

elements.title: Werth v. Taylor
elements.contents: ... that, under Werth v. Taylor, doctors are not always liable for giving blood transfusions to Jehovah's Witnesses who have explicitly refused them?

elements.title: Jay Som
elements.contents: ... that Jay Som created "1 Billion Dogs"?
